reviews.review_msg: he knows hes stuff. this  a good course for ppl that likes teamwork and presentations. about 46% of ur grade depends on ur teammembers, they will also grade u!! midterm and final VERY EASY as long as u read the book(dont have to crazily memeorize everything) and lectures. the avg for midterm was 85% shine in ur group and leave good impressions!

I graduated recently and thought of rating  professors that had an impact on my time at SFU and Rajiv was the first prof that came to mind. Truly inspiring, very knowledgeable and always ready to help. Don't miss!
Pushed me outside my comfort zone but was very happy that I decided to stick with it and did not drop after two weeks. Best course so far.

BUS710 Global Business Environments MBA course was academic and theoretical focused. Mostly reading outdated case studies to analyze, leaving much to be desired on how to practically advance forward given current state of global business. 

Friendly instructor, changed course requirements several times. Easy course but boring and didn't learn much.
Chill professor. Very knowledgeable. Ended up with a bad team but prof and TA helped me out of it.
Best course in 2026.

The professor was flexible with the grading criteria and addressed them. The lectures were very engaging, and I learned a lot from my classmates during in-class activities. Overall, the course was worth it.

Rajiv is chill and insightful. I found his lectures engaging, but unnecessary to attend. Take-home, online exam is chill. Two peer-reviewed papers to read each week (required reads as need to cite them on exam), with extremely vague syllabus and assignment instructions (its all a singular blurb on canvas) earn him the score deductions. He chill tho

Issues come from assignments. He gives a lot of room for creativity, however also means the rubric is going to be vague. Ur going to need to ask him questions to find guidance (super smart tho). Team assignments sucked a lot. Its like an IRL organization experiment. no motivation for team to come to class when team projects come you get spectators.

Amazing course!! Rajiv Kozhikode completely changed how I think about organizations. His original research is super engaging, and he gives you real freedom with memo assignments to explore what you find interesting. Trial assignments were super helpful too — great feedback and guidance. Super smart prof, super chill vibe. TAKE THIS CLASS!!

Rajiv's lectures are both engaging and highly informative. He makes class participation feel natural and is approachable, always patient, and thorough when clarifying doubts. Be prepared, though—this course involves a lot of reading and writing. That said, it's absolutely worth the effort. You will learn a lot.

If you are a person that learn concepts better with real-life examples, he is the right prof. Lectures always contains real life examples to explain the concept in real practice, and you can tell he is passionate. Would definitely take his class again if I can. Graded by 2 of 30% MT, 10% presentation, 20% project, and 10% participation

Rajiv is a good lecturer who knows the material quite well and seems to care about students learning objectives. However his course is graded quite competetively being it is an upper divsion course, and his lectures and partcipation is very vital for success. His instructions are very ambigous so be aware when preparing for midterms or projects!!!

Are you concentrating in HR? if not, stop reading now and look into 381.  RK is a great guy who really knows his stuff.  Course is challenging, especially for those who don't read English well (readings are academic meaning complex English use). Participation matters so you cannot be shy. Interesting course no doubt, but only take 374 if its needed

One of a kind course... Makes you rethink about things that you often take for granted about organizations. Exams are not easy even though they are open book (although technically there is no text book, only assigned readings). Despite this, I enjoyed the course. Definitely recommend.

At times, it was difficult to understand due to his accent + not using a mic in an echoing lecture hall. All exams are open book, and he highly encourages students' own interpretation of theories on  presentation which was great. Recommend this course to all accounting students the format is quite similar to BUS421.
Took her in the spring, so hard to catch up on what she delivered, and everything is so disorganized.

Steve-o rules. Sure he might think BUS374 is a load (as any intelligent business student should) and it might show in his effort, but I doubt any prof could make this nonsense (OB round 2) any more interesting. The presentations were fun too. Plus, if you were in his shoes, wouldn't you wanna teach a low maintenance course like this?

Steve is an ok teacher. The class is relatively easy but the group project was a disgrace since it was the first time he was teaching this class he had no idea how difficult the project was. The material in the class was not very difficult and Steve did an ok job teaching it but he dosent go out of his way to help anyone.
The worst Professor in SFU. Save your money and take your class with someone else. Not at all helpful.
no comments / discouraging ...

While the course material was pretty useless, I felt that the group activities he designed forces you to develop some people skills. He's approachable, he's down-to-earth, and really puts effort into his classes.

This class was alright. I enjoyed going to the 2 hour organization meetings, mostly because I had a really good looking girl in my group!!  The activities were pretty fun while the final was all mc and fair. The T.A however marked the written section for the midterm really hard.

1 hour lecture and 2 hours of interacting with group (of 15-20 people), that is a typical class for 374. interesting resume at first, but as the course developed, it was obvious that the lecture was unless and the activities fun nevertheless useless; easy credit (not so much if you have a bad groupo), avoid if one actually wants to learn something

I really like this guys personality, he's really confident (not cocky). The course is pretty hard if you end up with a bad group, but the class was overall fun, it was kind of like The Apprentice with all the "organizational" activities... again unless u have a bad group.
You really don't know how to teach, and you're so cocky man
Poor communication skills and the prof is very disorganized
Very professional and well prepared.

Tim's a pretty easy-going professor. There are a couple of group assignments in this course, but nothing super difficult. Exams are pretty straightforward as long as you pay attention to his slides. If you have a copy of the textbook that would really help you do well on the exams, although it is not mandatory.

Tim was a great professor. His midterm had a very low average, but it's on a curve so that doesn't really matter. Lots of easy assignments that increase your grade if you're willing to spend a little bit of time on them. Midterm and final exams both made you apply concepts, rather than memorizing details. Allowed cheat sheets for both.

One of the best profs in SFU. She's amazing, engaging, and very enthusiastic. She really helps you understand the concepts discussed in lecture through examples and even online methods. She fully utilizes SFU's online resources and even Facebook. Very helpful and takes criticism from students. Best course i've taken in SFU thus far.
Kirsten was a good instructor. I find the course challenging for high A and A+, but most people who put in decent meeting times can do well. Competitive groups make the course memorable.
I'm sorry some people are just not cut out of teaching. Prof Busche is one of them. This class was terrible and a total waste of money. I don't recommend it to anyone if he's teaching it!!!
Don't have to go to lecture...just look at power point slides and textbook will be sufficient to get a good mark on test...
lectures are very interesting... if you follow. Paper is challenging as its requirements are vague. But depend on who you have as a TA.
Four tests (15%), one paper(%40)...10% show up to lectures

Unless you have an undying passion for org theory, this class is zzzzzzz....but good thing is that you can zzzzzzz.  Just memorize all the words on the powerpoint slides and A+ it like me.  I'm still wondering how I did it!
He knows the stuff very well. But quizes are not easy. In 1st quiz, he will ask what he said in the lecture. Quizes are quite tricky.
Lecture is totally non related to midterms!!!

Interesting subject(if like HR and org theory).Lectures are almost too informative.Although grading format is simple, you need to learn everything and in detail.A challenge. Reading (book/ppt), studying is not optional.Paper is super hard.
His exam are crazy, his ppt notes are useless. Paper are really really unfiar because of ther marker AVOID HIM !!!!!!!
The quizz is very hard.Unreasonable expectation.

The most useless and boring lectures EVER! Don't even attend it. Study all the text and PPT slides for the quizes and it will be easy like hell. Then some good writing in your case study should give you an A range.
Easy quizzes, no final. Study the LECTURE NOTES!!
No Comments
EASY!!  Lectures are useless, quizzes are easy, paper is easy.  What more can you ask for?
No Comments
No Comments
Study those pwt notes! Lectures are optional (aka skipable)
Good course, weird, but good...
even geeks are skipping
the only way you can fail the exams is skipping them
Useless lectures, most people didn't even show up. Bad TA. Super easy.

Ginger is an amazing public speaker, she inspires and interests you immediately. Unfortunately, she rarely talked about the actual course topic, and the entire course had to be done through the textbook. Could have been an amazing class if Ginger had lectured on the topic.  Unfortunately instead it was boring with frustrating teamwork.

Ginger is a cook. First of all, all this class was was pure presenting. Every group presented every class and she didn't teach at all! Everything I learned (which was not much) was self-taught. Furthermore, she was very vague on what she expected. And we had to wait over a month for a grade on an assignment!

took this class because of the great things i heard about her...very disappointed. Group presentations every week, she barely speaks. Its a shame because she has great stories and experience and our $700 spent on this class would be better spent listening to her. Midterm/final easy,based on textbook...high averages though so watch out for the curve

Ok. Very interesting prof. Loves to talk about M&A's, and case-it's, and visual analytics, but not much on actual org. theory. Exams/midterms ENTIRELY textbook based, but none of that material covered in class. You still have to attend class though because your group may be called to present (expect only 1 pres. per half-semester).
Easy and straight forward, memorize all the stuff in the textbook and you will be fine.
great prof, got some funny stories,you'll get a chance to apply and analyze course concepts in group project, exmas are reasonable, work hard and you will get a good grade!
he is young and enthusiastic. the midterm was easy but the final was pretty tricky. He is very direct, and fair. i recommend him. great course.

Eric is a young, intelligent professor. He asks for feedback a lot - which I suppose is either a good or a bad thing, depending on your preference. The midterms was easy, but he adjusted the final to be harder based on student feedback. He has high interest with the course and he is approachable.

Quite possibly the worst professor I have come across in my time at SFU. Can't give clear instructions due to the fact that he doesn't know what he wants. Very inviting to discussions and objections until you actually try to do either with him.

Questions in the final exam are the worst questions I've ever seen in an exam. Questions are based on memorization. This is not the way you test in a business course. No application and no analyzing cases. It makes the teacher easier to make the questions. He is inexperienced and just made this course terrible.

Workload is high but, assignments are very manageable and easy to figure out. Midterm average was crazy low (as previously mentioned) but, marking is curved so, obviously if your an idiot compared to the class, your mark will reflect so... Lectures were decent as he doesn't simply read from his slides. Hint: Volunteer in class for extra marks!

Definitely not a great instructor, he gives very vague outline of what he wants done, then marks it extremely tough. There is a large workload in this class, a 3-page report due almost every week (after week 4), the first midterm average was 46%!!!
Worse of the worse. Thats all I have to say---and this is being nice. I'm taking bus 374 with him.....he's absolutely horrible!

Quizzes are ridiculously easy, MC/TF/short answer. Assigns 2 papers which are marked very generously (i.e. high avg) but I disliked the ambiguous guidelines for them. He is a charismatic guy with a lot of industry exp and uses it to help you understand the material. Often puts up his own money as prizes for in-class activities.

Mark's a good lecturer but I think the OB material is completely useless and I didn't attend the majority of lectures since the quizzes (midterms) were 90% based on text and 10% on in class videos. Average is really high, expect to need about 90+ for an A.
best professor ...take 374 with this prof if your English writing skill is not strong.  He's a easy marker.

quizzes, papers were relatively easy. the problem with that is that the class average is so high that after scaling, your good marks don't acutally translate to good letter grades. lectures are interesting, but you can just as easily do without the lectures by reading the textbook alone.
You won't regret having him as a prof!
A good prof indeed

A superb Prof. Very interested in org theory. Very interesting lectures. Easy quizzes, relevant paper, and an a very fun and informal final. Lots of industry experience. I liked the guest speakers too. He deserves the highest quality teaching award. 
 Thanks Mark, you are the coolest in the faculty.

Finally a prof who doesn't just regurgitate the textbook! Mark is a fantastic prof who draws on all sorts of disciplines to give students a better understanding of org theory and why it's important. Easy quizzes, relevant assignment, and fun final (or as close to fun as is possible)! Thanks for everything Mark!

Great prof.  Activities in lectures are fun and interactive.  Mark is very interested in the material and his enthusiasm for the course really shines through.  He brings in some interesting guest speakers too.
Fun lectures. Mark is a super nice and enthusiastic guy. Highly reccomended.
I totally recommend taking a class with Mark.  He's very interested in the materials... wonderful class.  Thanks Mark!!!
Nice guy, easy quiz and final. And easy marking organization anaylsis.
Super easy quiz, but gotta remember stuff well. Interesting lecture and he's a nice guy. Essays and exam questions are not hard as long as you support your answers well. Easy course and easy marks.
